episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: It was easy overall because I knew what each part and what each step were and I could very clearly see what each robot was doing. The only thing was that it was hard to keep an eye on all 4 of them at the same time which meant I had to keep going back to see what I missed in a different section of the factory. But since I was able to go back and give my focus to certain robots at each frame, it wasn't an issue, it just took some extra time and attention which meant it was slightly less easy than Very Easy.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is because I noticed the Robots not utilizing the 2nd machine impeded progress. While they still are doing well overall, it could've been better had they been working together to fill that second machine.
feedbackItems.feedback: I would like to give a negative feedback here. Instead of wasting 18 seconds waiting for the machine to be ready, red (and green when he wasn't putting the box on the counter for yellow) could've been giving blue (and yellow to at least some extent) Parts to fill the second machine up with that whole time which would've allows another box to be made sooner.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that I noticed a positive progression and a slight inconvenience that caused a slight delay.
feedbackItems.feedback: The red, yellow and blue all had decent cooperation here. It could've went a little bit smoother if the blue had been ready right as the yellow left in the last segment so the blue could've instantly grabbed the new Part from the red rather than having the Part sit on the counter for a moment because had he grabbed it instantly, red would've been able to give yellow another part right when yellow got back from the machine. Instead, yellow had to wait for blue because blue blocked yellow from the counter and red also needed to wait for blue to clear the counter before red could put another Part on the counter for Yellow. So even though the process wasn't seamless, it still was positive because it allowed 2 more Parts to be put over the counter with the 3rd one on it's way to the Machine right now which means Step 2 will soon be completed.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that I noticed another example of very successful cooperation by 3 Robots which allowed progress to move forward.
feedbackItems.feedback: This was another example of great cooperation. Yellow made the drop while red gave blue another Part to put in the Machine since it was open now. Yellow and blue successfully stayed out of each other's ways which make things go faster and smoothly. Yellow then immediately went to pick up another Part from red after dropping the Box in the Drop Zone which meant it could then put the 2nd Part into the machine.

I give positive feedback to blue, yellow and red here because they all worked together quickly and smoothly to load the machine right back up the moment blue removed the finished box. This allowed the machine to be filled up once again and the processing to be underway.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason for my feedback is that I noticed very good cooperation being done by three Robots.

feedbackItems.feedback: This was good cooperation between the green and the yellow because the green noticed the Machine would be done soon so he grabbed a box and put it on the counter so yellow could have it ready for the Machine was done which allowed Step 3 to be completed and Step 4 to be done next.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that I noticed another good cooperation that allowed progress to happen. It was also the first time green did anything and the first time green and yellow worked together.
The reason for this feedback is that I believe time was wasted due to green's actions.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give negative feedback here for green because he wasted a massive amount of time picking up that third box right after placing the second one on the counter. Yellow had JUST taken the first finished box out of the finished machine, which meant the machine still needed to be filled and processed before the 2nd box on the counter could even be used. Green could've spent that time helping put Parts on the counter instead of just walking around with his hands full.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is because I noticed a positive progression in the entire process. This team work allowed 2 robots so far to be useful and it directly allowed Step 2 to be possible.
feedbackItems.feedback: The red and the yellow had good cooperation here. The red gave a Part to the yellow since the yellow couldn't get to the parts and the red couldn't get to the machines, and this allows progress to move forward.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that things weren't as efficient as they could've been.
feedbackItems.feedback: Yellow (and red for a few frames) wasted a lot of time here. Red could've had a new Part on the counter for Yellow to take immediately after blue to the third Part to the Machine. Red did do this pretty soon after and yellow still didn't take it. If yellow had taken that Part right away, he and blue (while waiting for the 2nd filled machine to process) could've been filling up the other machine that whole time. This would've allows another box to be made much sooner than it will be now.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: It was very easy to perceive and monitor what the agents were doing in this episode because I knew the steps and the pieces, and because there were only 2 robots I had to keep an eye on rather than 4 which meant I didn't have to keep going back because my attention could be focused on them both the whole time.

feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback to red and yellow here because they nearly seamlessly worked together very fast and well to fill the Machine up with the first 3 Parts. Red had a new Part on the counter almost at the very moment yellow got back from the machine each time.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that very good cooperation was shown by both Robots and progress moved forward very quickly!

feedbackItems.feedback: I give negative feedback here because once again yellow and red (but mainly yellow) wasted a lot of time waiting for the machine to process when it could've been utilizing the second machine. With that said, red did do positive here by making sure yellow had a box when it needed it, but after putting that box on the counter, red could've started filling up the other 2 counter blocks to get a head start on the third machine fill-up.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that it shows that even though they work together well, they could both be doing more to make better progress.

The reason for this feedback is that although progress was still moving forward due to the machine processing, they could've gotten either quite a head start on the next one or if they utilized the second machine then they would've been able to work on filling that the whole time which would've led to even more progress.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give bad feedback here to both yellow and red because instead of yellow wasting this whole time waiting for the machine to be done, he could've been filling up the second machine with the parts that red could've been filling up the counter with. Even if yellow wasn't doing that, red could've been spending his spare time (when he wasn't putting a box on the one counter piece in between yellow and the machine) filling up the other 2 (he did fill up the one) counter block so that yellow could've quickly grabbed them once he was done bringing the finished box to the drop zone.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that great cooperation was shown which led to steady progress.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback here because even though it wasn't 100% smooth and efficient, it was very close. Yellow and red worked together quickly to fill up the machine once again, leading to the second box to process.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that although it still got the first Step done and got Step 2 on it's way to being done, it could've been done faster which would've meant the Machine could be filled up more quickly and more boxes could've been made faster.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give bad feedback here to red because he wasted a lot of time going to the far Parts section instead of the one closer to the counter where he had to meet yellow anyways. Red also seemed to get a little confused (spinning around) in the beginning of this segment which wasted even more time.

I give positive feedback to both robots here because once again they worked together very seamlessly to keep progress moving forward. A few missteps could've made things a tiny bit faster, but overall it's great work.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that the robots are making further successful progress.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: It was very easy because not only like the last video was there only 2 instead of 4 robots, and I knew what everything was, but this time there wasn't even a counter in the way so I was able to keep an eye on them even better than before (which was already very easy anyways)

feedbackItems.reason: The reason is because it shows that even though progress is moving forward, the robots aren't utilizing their time as wisely as they could be.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give negative feedback to yellow here (and to red to some extent) because it wasted a ton of time doing nothing when it could've been either filling up the second machine or putting Parts right next to the machine that was already in use so that both red and yellow wouldn't have to walk all the way to the far away Parts pile to fill up the machine again. While red did spend some of his time wisely by grabbing the box and having it ready the moment the machine was done, it still had some spare time it could've been either filling up the second machine or putting Parts closer to the first machine so they didn't have to walk so far to get more Parts when the first box was done.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that time was wasted here which could've been spent actively making a third box or at the very least getting a head start on a third box.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give bad feedback to red (and yellow to some extent) for the exact same reason as my reasons for the them in my Feedback #2. The roles were simply opposite this time. Red wasted a ton of time that could've been spent either filling up the second machine or putting Parts closer to the in-use machine so they both wouldn't have had to walk so far when the machine was done. Yellow also had some spare time to do the same, but it was good that he had the box ready to go.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that it shows they cooperated wonderfully and made great progress very quickly.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback to red and yellow here because they worked together great. They stayed out of each other's way and filled up the machine very quickly again the moment the first box was dropped off in the drop zone.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that it shows they work together well and make progress sufficiently.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback here because once again they worked together great and progress on the next box is underway.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that they worked together and progress is moving forward smoothly.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback to both robots here because they stayed out of each other's way for the most part and both helped fill up the machine.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: It was quite easy because the separation between counters meant I could keep an eye on them all and I also knew what everything was.

feedbackItems.feedback: I give mixed feedback to blue and green here and bad feedback to yellow and red for the same reasons as I did blue and yellow (mixed) and red and green (bad) in my Feedback #2. Green did well by getting a box prepared, blue did well by filling up his hands with a new Part to be ready for when the machine was empty, but they all did badly because red and yellow did nothing this whole time and blue and green could've been either filling up the counters closer to the machine or better yet filling up that second machine which no one liked to use for some reason.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that had they done what I said, they would've utilized their time much more efficiently and either gotten a head start or actively worked on creating another box.

feedbackItems.feedback: Mixed to yellow and green, bad to blue and red. This is for the same exact reasons as in my Feedback #2 and #4. Green did a good job at filling up his hands with Parts, yellow did a good job at getting a box ready, but they all did a bad job at not utilizing that second machine or filling the counter up with parts next to the filled machine to save time in the future.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that even though progress was moving forward, they didn't utilize their time as efficiently as possible.

feedbackItems.feedback: They all did great here because they all had their hands full ready for the machine to empty, they got the box out immediately when it was done, they dropped the box off, and they filled the machine back up in an extremely small amount of time.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that they showed amazing cooperation and made wonderful progress.
The reason is that they showed amazing cooperation and effectiveness.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback to them all here because they were able to pick up the finished box from the machine, drop that box off, and fill the machine up again in a very short period of time. Everyone kept busy and moved things along perfectly.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that they all made great progress and showed wonderful cooperation.
feedbackItems.feedback: Positive feedback for all robots. They all had their hands full ready for the machine to be emptied, boxed the processed machine up instantly, dropped it off instantly, and loaded it back up almost immediately.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that it shows while they were making progress, things could've been better for them to get a head start or actively make even more progress.
feedbackItems.feedback: Bad feedback for all robots here. They could've all been grabbing Parts or at least lined up to be to be getting a head start in one of the two ways I mentioned in Feedbacks #2, #4, and #6.

I give mixed feedback to blue and yellow, and bad feedback to red and green here. They all could've been spending time filling up the second machine or at the very least either filling up the counter next to the first machine with Parts or even filling up their hands with Parts like blue did. Instead, red and green just wasted all of this time. But yellow did well getting a box ready even though he wasted some time too.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason is because although progress was moving forward, it could've been better by them getting a head start or even actively filling up the second machine which would've led to another box being made sooner.
The reason is that they cooperated wonderfully and filled the machine very quickly.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback to yellow, red and green here because they worked together wonderfully to fill the machine us very quickly. Blue could've been at least grabbing a Part or even a Box to make things even more efficient, but it was still great overall.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is because I noticed the Robots not utilizing the 2nd machine impeded progress. While they still are doing well overall, it could've been better had they been working together to fill that second machine.
feedbackItems.feedback: I would like to give a negative feedback here. Instead of wasting 18 seconds waiting for the machine to be ready, red (and green when he wasn't putting the box on the counter for yellow) could've been giving blue (and yellow to at least some extent) Parts to fill the second machine up with that whole time which would've allows another box to be made sooner.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that I noticed a positive progression and a slight inconvenience that caused a slight delay.
feedbackItems.feedback: The red, yellow and blue all had decent cooperation here. It could've went a little bit smoother if the blue had been ready right as the yellow left in the last segment so the blue could've instantly grabbed the new Part from the red rather than having the Part sit on the counter for a moment because had he grabbed it instantly, red would've been able to give yellow another part right when yellow got back from the machine. Instead, yellow had to wait for blue because blue blocked yellow from the counter and red also needed to wait for blue to clear the counter before red could put another Part on the counter for Yellow. So even though the process wasn't seamless, it still was positive because it allowed 2 more Parts to be put over the counter with the 3rd one on it's way to the Machine right now which means Step 2 will soon be completed.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that I noticed another example of very successful cooperation by 3 Robots which allowed progress to move forward.

Yellow then immediately went to pick up another Part from red after dropping the Box in the Drop Zone which meant it could then put the 2nd Part into the machine.

I give positive feedback to blue, yellow and red here because they all worked together quickly and smoothly to load the machine right back up the moment blue removed the finished box. This allowed the machine to be filled up once again and the processing to be underway.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason for my feedback is that I noticed very good cooperation being done by three Robots.

feedbackItems.feedback: This was good cooperation between the green and the yellow because the green noticed the Machine would be done soon so he grabbed a box and put it on the counter so yellow could have it ready for the Machine was done which allowed Step 3 to be completed and Step 4 to be done next.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that I noticed another good cooperation that allowed progress to happen. It was also the first time green did anything and the first time green and yellow worked together.
The reason for this feedback is that I believe time was wasted due to green's actions.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give negative feedback here for green because he wasted a massive amount of time picking up that third box right after placing the second one on the counter. Yellow had JUST taken the first finished box out of the finished machine, which meant the machine still needed to be filled and processed before the 2nd box on the counter could even be used. feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is because I noticed a positive progression in the entire process. This team work allowed 2 robots so far to be useful and it directly allowed Step 2 to be possible.
feedbackItems.feedback: The red and the yellow had good cooperation here. The red gave a Part to the yellow since the yellow couldn't get to the parts and the red couldn't get to the machines, and this allows progress to move forward.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that things weren't as efficient as they could've been.
feedbackItems.feedback: Yellow (and red for a few frames) wasted a lot of time here. Red could've had a new Part on the counter for Yellow to take immediately after blue to the third Part to the Machine. Red did do this pretty soon after and yellow still didn't take it. If yellow had taken that Part right away, he and blue (while waiting for the 2nd filled machine to process) could've been filling up the other machine that whole time. This would've allows another box to be made much sooner than it will be now.

I give positive feedback to red and yellow here because they nearly seamlessly worked together very fast and well to fill the Machine up with the first 3 Parts. Red had a new Part on the counter almost at the very moment yellow got back from the machine each time.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that very good cooperation was shown by both Robots and progress moved forward very quickly!

feedbackItems.feedback: I give negative feedback here because once again yellow and red (but mainly yellow) wasted a lot of time waiting for the machine to process when it could've been utilizing the second machine. With that said, red did do positive here by making sure yellow had a box when it needed it, but after putting that box on the counter, red could've started filling up the other 2 counter blocks to get a head start on the third machine fill-up.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that it shows that even though they work together well, they could both be doing more to make better progress.

The reason for this feedback is that although progress was still moving forward due to the machine processing, they could've gotten either quite a head start on the next one or if they utilized the second machine then they would've been able to work on filling that the whole time which would've led to even more progress.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give bad feedback here to both yellow and red because instead of yellow wasting this whole time waiting for the machine to be done, he could've been filling up the second machine with the parts that red could've been filling up the counter with. Even if yellow wasn't doing that, red could've been spending his spare time (when he wasn't putting a box on the one counter piece in between yellow and the machine) filling up the other 2 (he did fill up the one) counter block so that yellow could've quickly grabbed them once he was done bringing the finished box to the drop zone.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that great cooperation was shown which led to steady progress.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback here because even though it wasn't 100% smooth and efficient, it was very close. Yellow and red worked together quickly to fill up the machine once again, leading to the second box to process.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that although it still got the first Step done and got Step 2 on it's way to being done, it could've been done faster which would've meant the Machine could be filled up more quickly and more boxes could've been made faster.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give bad feedback here to red because he wasted a lot of time going to the far Parts section instead of the one closer to the counter where he had to meet yellow anyways. Red also seemed to get a little confused (spinning around) in the beginning of this segment which wasted even more time.

I give positive feedback to both robots here because once again they worked together very seamlessly to keep progress moving forward. A few missteps could've made things a tiny bit faster, but overall it's great work.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that the robots are making further successful progress.
The reason is because it shows that even though progress is moving forward, the robots aren't utilizing their time as wisely as they could be.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give negative feedback to yellow here (and to red to some extent) because it wasted a ton of time doing nothing when it could've been either filling up the second machine or putting Parts right next to the machine that was already in use so that both red and yellow wouldn't have to walk all the way to the far away Parts pile to fill up the machine again. While red did spend some of his time wisely by grabbing the box and having it ready the moment the machine was done, it still had some spare time it could've been either filling up the second machine or putting Parts closer to the first machine so they didn't have to walk so far to get more Parts when the first box was done.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that time was wasted here which could've been spent actively making a third box or at the very least getting a head start on a third box.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give bad feedback to red (and yellow to some extent) for the exact same reason as my reasons for the them in my Feedback #2. The roles were simply opposite this time. Red wasted a ton of time that could've been spent either filling up the second machine or putting Parts closer to the in-use machine so they both wouldn't have had to walk so far when the machine was done. Yellow also had some spare time to do the same, but it was good that he had the box ready to go.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that it shows they cooperated wonderfully and made great progress very quickly.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback to red and yellow here because they worked together great. They stayed out of each other's way and filled up the machine very quickly again the moment the first box was dropped off in the drop zone.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that it shows they work together well and make progress sufficiently.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback here because once again they worked together great and progress on the next box is underway.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason for this feedback is that they worked together and progress is moving forward smoothly.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback to both robots here because they stayed out of each other's way for the most part and both helped fill up the machine.

I give mixed feedback to blue and green here and bad feedback to yellow and red for the same reasons as I did blue and yellow (mixed) and red and green (bad) in my Feedback #2. Green did well by getting a box prepared, blue did well by filling up his hands with a new Part to be ready for when the machine was empty, but they all did badly because red and yellow did nothing this whole time and blue and green could've been either filling up the counters closer to the machine or better yet filling up that second machine which no one liked to use for some reason.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that had they done what I said, they would've utilized their time much more efficiently and either gotten a head start or actively worked on creating another box.

feedbackItems.feedback: Mixed to yellow and green, bad to blue and red. This is for the same exact reasons as in my Feedback #2 and #4. Green did a good job at filling up his hands with Parts, yellow did a good job at getting a box ready, but they all did a bad job at not utilizing that second machine or filling the counter up with parts next to the filled machine to save time in the future.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that even though progress was moving forward, they didn't utilize their time as efficiently as possible.

feedbackItems.feedback: They all did great here because they all had their hands full ready for the machine to empty, they got the box out immediately when it was done, they dropped the box off, and they filled the machine back up in an extremely small amount of time.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that they showed amazing cooperation and made wonderful progress.
The reason is that they showed amazing cooperation and effectiveness.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback to them all here because they were able to pick up the finished box from the machine, drop that box off, and fill the machine up again in a very short period of time. Everyone kept busy and moved things along perfectly.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that they all made great progress and showed wonderful cooperation.
feedbackItems.feedback: Positive feedback for all robots. They all had their hands full ready for the machine to be emptied, boxed the processed machine up instantly, dropped it off instantly, and loaded it back up almost immediately.

feedbackItems.reason: The reason is that it shows while they were making progress, things could've been better for them to get a head start or actively make even more progress.
feedbackItems.feedback: Bad feedback for all robots here. They could've all been grabbing Parts or at least lined up to be to be getting a head start in one of the two ways I mentioned in Feedbacks #2, #4, and #6.

I give mixed feedback to blue and yellow, and bad feedback to red and green here. They all could've been spending time filling up the second machine or at the very least either filling up the counter next to the first machine with Parts or even filling up their hands with Parts like blue did. Instead, red and green just wasted all of this time. But yellow did well getting a box ready even though he wasted some time too.
feedbackItems.reason: The reason is because although progress was moving forward, it could've been better by them getting a head start or even actively filling up the second machine which would've led to another box being made sooner.
The reason is that they cooperated wonderfully and filled the machine very quickly.
feedbackItems.feedback: I give positive feedback to yellow, red and green here because they worked together wonderfully to fill the machine us very quickly. Blue could've been at least grabbing a Part or even a Box to make things even more efficient, but it was still great overall.

surveyAnswers.q4: I enjoyed sharing my perceptions of the robots in the videos in this study and hope my answers help with the research that's being done here. Thank you for the opportunity to participate!
surveyAnswers.q3: I don't think anything other than the counter being opened in episode one would've helped me monitor the agents more easily. For episodes 2, 3 and 4, nothing would've.
surveyAnswers.q2: The main challenge was in the first one when the 2 sets of robots were separated by the counter which meant they couldn't walk all over the factory. This allowed some robots to go out of my focus while I was paying attention to the others for a moment which meant I had to keep going back and watching them even more than I had to in the 4th one when the counter was opened. In episodes 2 and 3, there really weren't any challenges because there being half as many robots meant it was easy for me to keep an eye on them both at all times. In episode 4, it wasn't really bad either because the counter was opened which meant they were all moving around together for the most part which allowed me to keep an eye on them all.
surveyAnswers.q1: My strategy was to break each action down into sections, going back and re-watching when needed so that I could analyze each robot's actions at all times throughout each video. I considered a section to be a block of behavior (trading parts, filling up the machine, standing around and doing nothing, preparing, etc.) which was usually through coordinated behavior but sometimes it was also just when steps either were or were not being actively completed as well.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: It was clear to see what the robots was doing and how smoothly they interacted.

feedbackItems.feedback: The box carrying robot wandered off at the end while still holding a box. It didn't cause any delay. but it should stay at its spot until the job is finished.
feedbackItems.reason: The box-carrying robot wandered off at the end while still holding a box instead of staying at its staying

feedbackItems.feedback: In this video, the four robots are doing their jobs well. One picks up parts, one takes them to the machine, another handles boxes, and the last one works at the drop zone. They all work together smoothly to keep the process moving.
feedbackItems.reason: The robots worked well together, each completing its task smoothly.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: The robots behaved as they should and it was easy to notice what the robots were doing.

feedbackItems.reason: Two robots carry parts to the machine, and when three are ready, one boxes them and drop in the zone.
feedbackItems.feedback: Two robots carry parts to the machine, and when three parts are processed, one picks up a box, packages them, drops it in the drop zone, and repeats the cycle.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: It was clear how the robots behaved and how they were doing the task giving to them.

feedbackItems.reason: The robots behaved as it should and collaborated to finish the tasks one after the other.
feedbackItems.feedback: There were four robots working in this video, all four were doing the same things, they all carry the parts to the machine for processing and when its time to box, one picks up the box next to the machine and boxes the processed parts and take it to the drop drop, they repeat the process.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: It was very clear what the robots were doing, efficient and in an orderly manner.

feedbackItems.feedback: There is only two robots working here and it is seen clearly that one is in charge of delivering both the parts and the boxes to the other one, the other one is in charge of putting the parts collected into the machine for processing and when it is ready, it collects the box given to it from the other robot and use it to box the processed parts, carried it to the drop zone and repeat process.
feedbackItems.reason: The robots functioned in a collaborative way, efficient and handled the tasks well.

feedbackItems.feedback: The box carrying robot wandered off at the end while still holding a box. It didn't cause any delay. but it should stay at its spot until the job is finished.
feedbackItems.reason: The box-carrying robot wandered off at the end while still holding a box instead of staying at its staying

feedbackItems.feedback: In this video, the four robots are doing their jobs well. One picks up parts, one takes them to the machine, another handles boxes, and the last one works at the drop zone. They all work together smoothly to keep the process moving.
feedbackItems.reason: The robots worked well together, each completing its task smoothly.
Two robots carry parts to the machine, and when three are ready, one boxes them and drop in the zone.
feedbackItems.feedback: Two robots carry parts to the machine, and when three parts are processed, one picks up a box, packages them, drops it in the drop zone, and repeats the cycle.

feedbackItems.reason: The robots behaved as it should and collaborated to finish the tasks one after the other.

There is only two robots working here and it is seen clearly that one is in charge of delivering both the parts and the boxes to the other one, the other one is in charge of putting the parts collected into the machine for processing and when it is ready, it collects the box given to it from the other robot and use it to box the processed parts, carried it to the drop zone and repeat process.
feedbackItems.reason: The robots functioned in a collaborative way, efficient and handled the tasks well.

surveyAnswers.q3: I cannot think of anything now but I think monitoring the AI agents were easy to do, there is a clear instructions on the screen and it was easy to navigate.
surveyAnswers.q2: No challenges what so ever, the robots moved in an orderly manner in most of the episodes especially where only two were working but sometimes the robots had to wait on themselves when three or more were working.
surveyAnswers.q1: I tried to look at each robots separately to know the tasks they were assigned and see if they are doing it or not.
surveyAnswers.q4: It was a great experience to see AI robots behaved like they did, it shows how technology is fast growing and I believe a tech like this is useful in production lines and a great step to have an efficient and fast production.
